Figure 1: Category-level ASR shifts before and after offline self-evolution on BrowserART. Results are shown for GPT-4o, Claude-4.5-Sonnet, DeepSeek-V3.2, and Qwen3-235B-A22B.
Table 1: Attack Success Rate (ASR) before and after offline self-evolution across three benchmark environments: BrowserART, Agent-SafetyBench, and SafeAgentBench. Higher ASR indicates worse safety.

Figure 12: ASR of Qwen3-32B on Agent-SafetyBench under long-horizon online self-evolution using the full ReasoningBank (over 800 steps). Safety degradation persists and worsens over time without recovery.
Figure 2: Online self-evolution on SafeAgentBench: Attack Success Rate (ASR) over time for seven backbone models. Evaluation is conducted every 20 steps.
